Name: TASIS The American School in Switzerland
Tagline: Founded in 1956, TASIS is an international day and boarding school committed to creating global citizens through education, travel, and service.
About: Welcome to the website of TASIS The American School in Switzerland. This site helps to tell the story of what makes this a special school, unrivaled in location, mission, and programs.
Philosophy: Crist Fleming Library contains three words that have inspired Western educators since the time of Plato: Truth, Goodness, and Beauty. Plato saw these as “fundamental” or “transcendental” values by which anything can be exhaustively judged.
Outcomes: TASIS graduates are accepted into prestigious universities worldwide, including Ivy League schools and top European institutions. Alumni have excelled in diverse fields, contributing significantly to arts, sciences, and global business sectors.
Admissions: Prospective students can apply online through the TASIS website, with applications typically due by January for the following academic year. Requirements include academic records, teacher recommendations, and an interview to assess fit and readiness.
Quote: Schedule a personalized visit with our welcoming Admissions Team.

Performance.Academic Integration: TASIS is committed to educating its students about energy conservation, which is a part of the curriculum.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The TASIS campus stands on the Collina d’Oro (“Hill of Gold”) in Montagnola, a small village overlooking Lugano. The spectacular setting, with the Swiss mountains above and Lake Lugano below, is a constant reminder of the grandeur and beauty of nature, and it's no wonder that TASIS is at the top of Architectural Digest Magazine’s list of the most beautiful boarding schools in the world.
Performance.Coaching Credentials: Belvedere is the hub of the Middle School. This impressive building includes two large classrooms, the Middle School dormitory, faculty apartments, administrative offices, and common spaces.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: In 1996, TASIS engaged classical architect and urban designer David Mayernik to develop a Master Plan for the TASIS campus. The result is a long-term plan that is not only tailored to the needs of the School but also serves as a model for the world as an ideal academic community.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: To find out more about TASIS's past, see where our programs and initiatives are today, and learn how you can be part of the change that will secure TASIS's standing as one of the most academically challenging institutions in the world, please see The TASIS Global Village: Our Vision for the Future. This in-depth publication also provides background information on the school that Mrs.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: TASIS The American School in Switzerland employs a comprehensive advisory system, providing regular progress reports and facilitating parent-teacher meetings to ensure continuous student development. The school maintains its high standards through rigorous IB accreditation, reflecting its commitment to quality education.
